---
title: "Fish-PACE User Group"
favicon: "favicon.ico"
image: "images/pace_emblem_round_900w.png"
links:
  - label: Slack
    url: "https://fish-pace.slack.com/"
  - label: GitHub
    url: "https://github.com/fish-pace"
  - label: JupyterHub
    url: "https://cryointhecloud.com/"
  - label: Email
    url: "mailto:eli.holmes@noaa.gov"
output:
  postcards::trestles
site: distill::distill_website
---

The Fish-PACE User Group serves as a forum for connecting, supporting, and growing the fisheries and ocean ecosystem user community around NASA’s NASA PACE mission and emerging ocean color products, with the goal of advancing applications for fisheries, marine ecosystems, and coastal decision-making. We support forums, events and trainings (workshops and hackweeks) to support fisheries and marine ecosystem applications for PACE data.

## Upcoming Events

* June 19 - Fish-PACE Onboarding: Intro to earthaccess Python package and on-boarding to the [CryoCloud JupyterHub](https://cryointhecloud.com/) for Fish-Pace UG members. [Register](https://zoom.us/meeting/register/_gdC2qNeRQqtyM9e0U1GgQ). All welcome!
* [CoastWatch Satellite Summer 201](https://coastwatch-training.github.io/trainings/upcoming/sat201_26.html) July 10, 17, 24 and 31, 2026, Virtual 11am - 1pm (Pacific Time). July 17th session is Ryan Vandermeulen presenting on PACE products beyond Chlorophyll for fisheries and ocean ecosystems applications.
[Register](https://forms.gle/XGtZWLqMAueXZQZX9)
* [OceanHackWeek 2026](https://oceanhackweek.org/ohw26/) will be August 24-28, 2026 at the Bamfield Marine Sciences Centre in BC, Canada. Applications open May 15. As in the past 2 years, Eli Holmes will mentor a Fish-PACE focused project. Great event for early career scientists.

## Past Events

* [Fish-PACE HackWeek Jan 2026](https://fish-pace.github.io/hackweek-2025/) Virtual Jan 26-30, 2026
* [EDMW 2025](https://nmfs-opensci.github.io/EDMW-EarthData-Workshop-2025/) Introduction to using earth data in the cloud for scientific workflows: PACE hyperspectral ocean color data
* [HyperDawg](https://github.com/ocean-satellite-tools/hyperdawg): Bi-weekly informal discussions and experimentation
* [NOAA Fisheries Friday HackHours](https://nmfs-opensci.github.io/NMFSHackDays-2026/): Though not hyperspectral focused, much of the training is relevant to working with hyperspectral data.
